personality_one_liner: A trustworthy, premium, and warm hyper-local marketplace that feels like a polished native iOS utility, replacing cold corporate blues with inviting terracotta and stone tones.

spacing.rule: Generous vertical breathing room. Cards are never flush to edges, use 'lg' padding. Stick to single column lists for provider cards.

do's: Bottom tab bar (must have a translucent tinted base underneath).
Sticky headers when scrolling over provider detail pages.
Hero category cards with photo backdrops.
dont's: Form inputs, basic list rows, body content.

screens.name: Home (Discovery)
screens.purpose: Allow users to instantly find and filter trusted local service providers.

states.loading: Skeleton UI for hero card and category chips.
states.empty: Illustration of a quiet neighborhood + 'No providers available in your area yet.'
states.error: Message: 'Unable to load local services.' + Retry button.

screens.layout: Custom Safe Area header with user location. Prominent search bar. Horizontal scrollable chips for Categories (Home Repairs, EV Charging, Cleaning). Followed by a vertical list of 'Top Rated Providers' using single-column cards filling the horizontal space. Sticky bottom tab bar (glass).

screens.name: Provider Detail
screens.purpose: Build trust by showcasing the provider's stats, reviews, and portfolio.
screens.layout: Full-bleed hero image at the top with a top-to-bottom gradient scrim (Layer 0: image, Layer 1: linear-gradient, Layer 2: Provider name and rating). Below hero: 'About', 'Services Offered', 'Reviews'. Sticky primary CTA at the bottom ('Book Service') overlapping the scroll view with safe-area clearance.

screens.name: Booking Flow & Checkout
screens.purpose: A seamless, multi-step process to finalize scheduling and payment.

states.error: Inline form errors or Stripe payment failure message.

screens.layout: Step 1: Scoping/Notes. Step 2: Date/Time selection (calendar view). Step 3: Checkout. Labels above inputs. Fixed CTA at the bottom inside KeyboardAvoidingView.

screens.name: Bookings History
screens.purpose: Track current and past service requests.

states.empty: Illustration + 'You haven't booked any services yet.'
states.error: Message: 'Could not fetch your bookings.' + Retry button.

screens.layout: Sticky glass header with a Segmented Control (Active / Past). Vertical list of cards. Each card displays service type, provider name, date, and a prominent status pill (e.g., Requested, Accepted, En route, Completed).

screens.name: AI Chat Assistant
screens.purpose: Help users describe their problem and get recommended services immediately.

states.empty: Welcome message: 'Describe what you need help with.'

screens.layout: Standard chat interface. Messages from AI in surfaceTertiary, messages from user in brandPrimary. Suggestions displayed as horizontal scrollable chips above the text input.

screens.name: Profile & Dashboard
screens.purpose: Manage settings and toggle between Customer and Provider mode.
screens.layout: List of settings rows with tinted icon containers. Prominent toggle for 'Provider Mode'. In Provider Mode: dashboard shows upcoming jobs, earnings summary, and an 'Available' toggle switch.

hero_home_repair.alt: Close-up of exposed plumbing pipes with rustic textures on a construction site wall

category_ev_charging.alt: Public charging station with eco-friendly design for electric vehicles

hero_home_cleaning.alt: A clean and organized home office desk with cleaning products

instructions_to_main_agent: NEVER use blues, indigo, or purple. The primary brand color is Terracotta/Rust (#C65D47) with a warm stone-white background (#FAFAF9) to convey trust, warmth, and premium native feel.
Implement the 'Sticky primary CTA over scrolling content' rule for the 'Book Service' and 'Confirm Booking' screens. The button must be anchored at the bottom outside the scroll view.

Ensure the Provider Detail hero image uses the MANDATORY gradient-to-dark scrim pattern (Layer 1: expo-linear-gradient from transparent to ≥70% surfaceInverse at bottom) so the white text is completely legible.
Use Phosphor icons consistently across all screens, adhering to the rule of one weight/style per screen (except the bottom tab bar).
For cards in the Home list, use a single column layout where width='100%' minus the 'lg' padding. Do not use 48% flex blocks that leave empty space.
In the Profile/Settings screen, use tinted icon containers for every row (icon inside a brandTertiary rounded square).
Include responsive logic: although built in React Native Expo, the flex layouts and single column max-widths must adapt gracefully to web view.
